title: Configuration: Setting the Static Folder Path in Your Flask App

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll learn how to configure the static folder path in your Flask application. \
This is an important step in serving static assets like CSS, JavaScript, and image files.

By default, Flask looks for static files in a folder named "static" in your project directory. \
However, you can customize this location to suit your project structure.

To set a custom static folder path, you can use the 'static_folder' parameter when initializing your Flask app. Here's an example:

```python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__, static_folder='my_static_files')
```

In the next 2 worked examples - we will see how to serve CSS from the default 'static' folder as well as our custom 'styles' folder.
